item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so necro it won't be seen but…

Depending on how the anti gravity machine works, it could be possible that just turning it upside down could make it function correctly. Which is just the sort of obvious thing a guy like this would miss.

+1 btw. Loved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was interesting. I still don’t understand why it’s so anomalously highly rated, though. Going to keep my vote neutral since I don’t want much greater pieces in the 2000-2100 series to be overshadowed (which I decided to read upwards for no particular reason).

I believe there are a few annoying clichés (GoI’s that serve no purpose, abuse of lists with quirky locations, etc.). I didn’t manage to enter actual suspension of disbelief due to the above. Also, even though I’ve been reading SCPs for some time, I still lack the knowledge of lore that seems necessary to understand the final letter. Guess that’s more of a wink to fans of that particular canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Electricity is supplied via a number of interconnected powerstrips. Following the powerstrips ultimately leads to a final power strip plugged into itself. Unplugging it removes power from the entire facility.

I enjoyed the whole thing, but this line was where I knew it was going to be a +1.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Good idea. I'm making the edits now, I'll do that.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: The Chaos Insurgency, Serpents hand, and Manna Charitable Foundation also all get name dropped, so linking to their hubs might be a good idea.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Most likely, unless HGW's suggestion of consulting with EB for flux correction helped.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Too old.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: The U-HEC sounds like a good link, I'll edit it in now. Though, on the subject of giant robots, SCP-2406 seems like a giant robot, but I don't think it's applicable here.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: My thinking there was they say they don't know where it cane from, and being the pathologically compartmentalized CI could very well have bought something without knowing it…

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: I would veto the 2490 crosslink because it implies that the CI purchased 2490, which clashes with the story in 2490’s article itself.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
I think the giant robot link should lead to something involving actual giant robots and not human-sized androids, Anderson's paratech mainly is in advanced "AI" (implied to be derived from human consciousness) and robotic systems — not in physics defying engineering. Something like MTF Eta-5 or to the GOC's U-HEC "Orange Suits" either, or both, of which might use technology derived from Professor V.

Also the section about the Chaos Insurgency seems a good place for some links:

Sold them one or two things,

for example, but if the authors of those don't like that connection, there are several other possibilities.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

There are a few places where I think other mechanical, science-y anomalies could be linked. After all, he is a mad scientist who's had many dealings with many GoI's, I don't see why not.

Editing this:

Six-meter-tall humanoid robot made primarily of chrome and steel.

To this:

Six-meter-tall humanoid robot made primarily of chrome and steel.

Editing this:

A bin labeled "cyborg parts." Inside were a number of artificial limbs, sensory organs, and other body parts.

To this:

A bin labeled "cyborg parts." Inside were a number of artificial limbs, sensory organs, and other body parts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glorious V skink protocol!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For a while, I always knew of the link to the Wanderer's Library article but I don't see its significance.

Anyway, this is a cool article and I love the character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Imagine if this became SCP-2000, and it acted as a major influence for the site in its place.

In all seriousness, though, this is amazing. It made me chuckle at every turn, and I loved the characterization used.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
lmao can't believe how long it took me to notice the wanderer's library link. hidden in plain sight!

still don't really get the hidden meaning to this article though, I think it's time for me to accept I'm a dummy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason, the voice he has in my head is Wheatley's. Like, Portal Wheatley. Still gets a +1 because holy frick this is ridiculous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
He probably took too many mentats.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Brilliant. Absolutely loved it. In fact, this made it to my list of Favorite SCPs.
It is odd, and funny, and creative at the same time.
What possibly can you want more?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. It pulls off the silliness quite well, and its nice to read an scp that has an element of cute and fun once in a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's what brains crave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love this one. Was anyobdy else reminded of the Old World Blues DLC for Fallout New Vegas?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … Is this guy's brain being kept alive in Brawndo?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was such a fun article to read. I can't think of anything I didn't like. It was silly, interesting, farfetched, and detailed. I could almost hear the brain speaking. He just had so much personality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Probably for the best; at some point it'd probably have ended up making giant pigeons or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love this one. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brain in a jar? LOVE IT! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can think of literally nothing about this SCP I don't love. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love i am jack's lack of osha compiance

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Exactly my idea. Mentats for all! Upvoted.

Kept imagining Dr. Klein or Dr. Mobius doing the voice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was imagining it in their voices while reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good, +1, although shame the soup kitchen couldn't keep the enlarger lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: TL333s was saying that he would have upvoted +11; as in, at 11 parts in the article he would have upvoted. (I think.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Someone above me said this was worth a +11. That is ridiculous. At the very least it's a +25. Maybe more. Of course, since this site obviously has bugs, a +1 will suffice.

Seriously though, this skip is a perfect blend of silly and more silly. Absolutely adore this. Though the fact that the retrieval is before the description annoys the fuck out of me. Still awesome though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alternate reality Cave Johnson wherein Caroline never existed, leaving nobody behind to take his place, so they put his brain in a jar to attempt to reanimate him, but only brought his inventive part back?

That's a stupid idea. Your idea is great though! Good job getting into the early-access 2k's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Chaos Insurgency.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's just because I'm really tired, but I can't seem to remember what CI stands for right now. What is it supposed to mean?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get that it has to do with the key that was found. Is it an additional key for SCP-004 or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i am jack's lack of osha compiance

upvoteneered

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Genius was the first thing I was thinking of while reading this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[19:10] DrMann Okay, I've made what will hopefully be the last edit to Brain in a Jar.
[19:11] DrMann It is even /more/ infuriatingly subtle.
[19:12] thedeadlymoose " It is even /more/ infuriatingly subtle"
[19:12] thedeadlymoose Mann
[19:12] thedeadlymoose I seem to recall having a conversation with you
[19:12] thedeadlymoose that pertains to this
[19:12] thedeadlymoose in fact I believe it pertained to this very thing
[19:13] thedeadlymoose WAIT OH MY GOD
[19:13] thedeadlymoose Bahahahahaha.
[19:13] thedeadlymoose I take it all back.
[19:14] thedeadlymoose claps
[19:14] DrMann bows.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Made one last small change. Very small.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was absolutely not my intention.

But it fits so goddamned well…

I'm tempted to have a tuna fish sandwich labeled "tiger bait" on the shelves.

Mind, I will say that the intention wasn't that he simply crossed off "anti" turning it into a gravity generator. Rather, the intention is that he was trying to build an anti-gravity generator, got something a little bit wrong, and said, "Eh, screw it," and just crossed out "anti."

But yeah, I do love your interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And they didn't allow me to have any dinosaurs. What kind of totalitarian wasteland doesn't allow dinosaurs?

He also liked dinosaurs, yes?

I really like this theory, and I hope it's true.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Interesting Theory: this guy reminds me of Calvin, from Calvin and Hobbes, when he grows up. I could see him just writing TELEPORTER on the side of a cardboard box and have it work as an actual teleporter.

While I doubt this was your intent, I think you'll like this angle as there are several things leading to this theory:

-The anti-gravity device that works as a gravity device when its crossed out. This is how calvin's cardboard box machines work.
-Same with the box of guns
-Calvin went through a cloning phase
-Calvin most definitely went through a dinosaur phase
-Calvin could "visit" the moon in a rocket without leaving the house.
-They both a lot talk
-They both talk about a lot of psudoscience that just works for no reason.
-People have compared Calvin to Fight Club, hence the Fight Club reference

So… actually, after writing them all down, this is Calvin grown up, and his imagination actually works for real. The only thing its missing is a subtle tiger reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hate that the description doesn't begin with a description. It's a pet peeve.
If it wasn't for that, I would have up-voted without reservation.
As it is, I just barely came down positive because of the great patter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I noticed a couple possibly meaningful initials in the example note amid the more obvious call outs. Am I on the right track?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not…quite sure what place a Fight Club reference has in this article, this coming from somebody who knows (somewhat) what's going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've made some changes. Hopefully this will help key people in on the secret.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm interested to see that the articles' interesting secrets — particularly one key to the article — are not being noticed by most users. Or any, perhaps.

This doesn't mean that the readers are stupid — it may well mean that they are too hidden. I, after all, know about them because I was told.

Based on that, I would advise considering making things a touch less hidden. Although I like the article either way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just read bits of this article to my mother and had the following conversation with her.

"How does a brain in a jar build things?"

"Oh, he's got little robots that do things for him."

"Where did the robots come from?"

"He built them."

"Yes, but how?"

"Using his notes!"

green food coloring, and artificial flavoring

This by itself is worth an upvote, even over the mixed-up recovery vs. description locations (which really was the only downside to this article). After reading through all the notes and -Bs (can I just stop and point out that this brain created a goddamn ninja robot gun?) and recovery logs, I think you're up to at least a +11 from me. Pity it doesn't go that high.

And I thought Space Wizard was weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll upvote this.
Or I think I already upvoted it.
Wait, let me check my notes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
713 different laser guns (in a bin labeled "713 different laser guns").
Never will look at mashed potatoes the same way
Weaponized snails

This is just nice. Yep, first thoughts that came to mind were of a serious Wondertainment. Just your good old mad scientist, inventing stuff that sure is great, but we don't really understand HOW.

I really like the execution of the idea, typical to Mann. Even though I didn't like a lot the last idea with the marble tunnel, what struck in my mind was once again the execution. As for the jar filled with strange stuff to support his brain, along with complex mechanical things that couldn't work by any means by modern science, I thought - he is a reality bender! A guy that makes a complex gizmo and imbues it with powers of funcion my some kind of reality bender magic. I imagine his voice to be some kind of a stereotypized Albert Einsten in a comedy movie.
The selling of anomalous items to various GoIs reminded me of The Lost Room.

What I miss about is proposal is a sense of graveness. Engaging the reader with serious thought about the scary implications of the SCP. Bigfoot as the 1000 proposal did it well, Ihpkmn's proposal does it also very well. This is an average idea well executed with some funny undertones that I would heartily upvote if it was on the mainlist, but as 2000, I cannot support it with my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it. It was amusing, it was interesting, it was intriguing, and it had more layers than an onion. It was very well-written, and overall enjoyable to read.

Additionally, it's nice to see that not everything in the Foundation-verse really has a purpose. This guy just does crazy things and makes impossible things…because he wants to. Not for other's enjoyment, not for future use, not even for profit. Just because he can and is able to.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy crap…he has been in or has had dealings with everyone short of the CI and the Foundation. And even then they've both had indirect dealings with him. The CI likely took some of his deliveries and the Foundation likely has some of his stuff in containment somewhere. Man…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't particularly like the SCP-2000-B being a big random grab-bag of crap.

Actually as soon as I was finished typing that out, I realized I love the idea of a big random grab-bag of crap like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, I really do, it's silly-but-not-too-silly, it's interesting, I'd +1 it so hard if this wasn't for 2000. It just doesn't seem very…. dynamic. It's a good SCP, a great SCP, but it's not bigfoot-level creepy-cool-awe-inspiring amazing. No vote, will +1 when I see this in series 3.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You made me laugh hard with the gatorade. The pun also made me thirsty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This needs to happen. It's been too long since the last Blackwood tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sounds like a Genius: The Transgression character to me.

Also, this guy should team up with Lord Blackwood sometime.
